objects.name: Analytic 1731
objects.description: An application performs repeated symmetric cryptographic operations (e.g., AES/RC4) on collected or staged data using locally accessible or reusable keys, followed by structured outbound communication. Detection correlates symmetric crypto API invocation + key reuse patterns + data staging + background execution context + network transmission, especially when inconsistent with expected application functionality.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Symmetric key material reused across multiple encryption operations within short interval OR derived locally without secure hardware-backed storage

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App invokes symmetric encryption routines (e.g., AES/RC4 cipher initialization + encrypt operations) with repeated key usage across multiple data buffers

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App writes high-entropy encrypted blobs to local storage or memory buffers prior to transmission

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Crypto + data staging occurs while app_state=background OR device_locked=true OR no recent user interaction

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: App not in enterprise-approved list performing network + crypto behavior inconsistent with declared functionality

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Time correlation between symmetric encryption operations and outbound communication
Threshold for detecting encrypted payloads based on entropy scoring
Number of repeated uses of the same symmetric key within a defined interval
Apps expected to use symmetric encryption (e.g., messaging, VPN)
Whether encryption activity should occur only during active user interaction
Expected jitter vs periodic encrypted communication
